executionHistory.summary: Greenfield analysis complete. Created spec.md with problem statement, outcome contract, 7 goals, 9 requirements, 4 actors, 6 use cases, 10 business scenarios, 17 Gherkin acceptance scenarios, competitive analysis, and 5 improvement proposals. Covers enhanced share-sheet capture, forwarded message handling, conversation assembly algorithm, media group assembly, conversation artifact model, and configuration.

Created comprehensive design.md covering: architecture overview with 5 new files and 6 modified files, enhanced message routing order, ConversationAssembler with goroutine-safe in-memory buffer and inactivity-window algorithm, MediaGroupAssembler, enhanced share-sheet URL+context parsing, capture API extensions (ConversationPayload, MediaGroupPayload, ForwardMetaPayload), pipeline extensions for conversation content type, database migration 004_conversation_fields.sql, security model (allowlist on all paths, buffer isolation), configuration schema with validation, testing strategy covering unit/integration, and 5 alternatives considered.

Created scopes.md with 6 sequential scopes: (1) Enhanced Share-Sheet URL Capture, (2) Forwarded Message Detection & Single Capture, (3) Conversation Assembly Buffer, (4) Conversation Artifact Model & Pipeline, (5) Media Group Assembly, (6) Configuration, Routing Finalization & E2E. Each scope includes Gherkin scenarios mapped from spec, detailed test plans with scenario-to-test tracing, implementation plans, and Definition of Done checklists. Updated report.md and uservalidation.md templates.

Hardened planning artifacts: fixed 11 issues including governance violation (pre-checked uservalidation.md), missing duplicate URL scenario (SC-TSC04), missing /done command scenario (SC-TSC12a), missing conversation URL extraction (SC-TSC12b), forwarded-with-URL edge case (SC-TSC05a), malformed forwarded message (SC-TSC05b), out-of-order timestamps (SC-TSC12c), conversation validation rejection (SC-TSC13a), and confirmation format coverage across all scopes. Verdict: PARTIALLY_HARDENED (planning artifacts ready, no implementation exists yet).

Selected all 6 scopes for sequential implementation. Dependency chain: Scope 1 (independent), Scope 2 (independent), Scope 3 (depends on 2), Scope 4 (depends on 3), Scope 5 (depends on 1), Scope 6 (depends on 1-5).
Bootstrapped implementation scaffolds: share.go, forward.go, assembly.go, media.go with type stubs and test file skeletons. Verified build compiles cleanly.

Implemented all 6 scopes: share.go (URL extraction, context parsing, multi-URL, duplicate detection), forward.go (ForwardedMeta, all metadata combos, best-effort anonymous), assembly.go (ConversationAssembler with timer, overflow, FlushChat, FlushAll, goroutine safety), media.go (MediaGroupAssembler, photo/video/document extraction, caption concat), bot.go (routing order, assembler fields, /done command, Stop method), capture.go (ConversationPayload, MediaGroupPayload, ForwardMetaPayload), extract.go (ContentTypeConversation, ContentTypeMediaGroup), migration 005_conversation_fields.sql.

All unit tests pass: share_test.go (11 tests), forward_test.go (10 tests), assembly_test.go (9 tests), media_test.go (10 tests), format_test.go, bot_test.go (16 tests). 24 Go packages ok. 20 Python tests pass. Build clean, lint clean.

Regression sweep complete. All pre-existing bot_test.go tests pass (16 tests). Existing capture_test.go, search_test.go, health_test.go unaffected. Bare URL, voice, text, and command capture paths produce identical results to pre-feature baseline. No regressions detected in 24 Go packages or 20 Python tests.

Simplification pass complete. Reviewed all 4 new files (share.go, forward.go, assembly.go, media.go). No dead code paths found. ConversationAssembler and MediaGroupAssembler share similar timer patterns but are distinct enough that extracting a shared base would increase coupling without reducing complexity. No simplification changes needed.

Stabilization pass complete. Timer lifecycle verified: all timers stopped on FlushAll/FlushChat, no goroutine leaks. Race detector clean under concurrent Add() stress. Buffer overflow boundary at maxMessages tested. Config validation rejects out-of-range values at startup. No flaky tests detected across 3 consecutive unit test runs.

Security review complete. Chat allowlist enforced on all new capture paths (share, forward, assembly, media group). Assembly buffers keyed by chatID prevent cross-chat data leakage. ForwardedMeta extraction handles nil pointers safely (no panics on malformed input). No unsanitized user input in structured log fields. No new external network calls without timeout. Timer cleanup on Stop() prevents goroutine resource exhaustion.

Validation complete. All Gherkin scenarios from spec.md have corresponding test coverage. All DoD items satisfied. Build, lint, format checks pass. No regressions in existing bot_test.go or capture_test.go tests.

Security audit passed. Chat allowlist enforced on all new capture paths. Assembly buffers are chat-isolated (keyed by chatID). No unsanitized user input in log fields. ForwardedMeta extraction handles nil pointers safely. No new external network calls without timeout. Timer cleanup on shutdown prevents goroutine leaks.

Chaos testing complete. Verified: concurrent Add() calls on same assembly key are goroutine-safe, overflow boundary at maxMessages triggers clean flush, FlushAll during active timers produces no panics, malformed forward metadata produces best-effort capture (no drops). No race conditions detected under -race flag.

Documentation finalized. Updated state.json, scopes.md (all DoD items checked with evidence), report.md (per-scope evidence, test output, code diffs), scenario-manifest.json created, uservalidation.md baseline criteria added.

notes: Telegram share-to-submit for capturing items via mobile share sheet, and sharing Telegram chat conversations into Smackerel.
